description: Mobile web phase A5: the unsupported-combination SKIP matrix on iOS, asserted as behavior. Safari is the one supported iOS browser; every other engine SKIPs deterministically at the mobile-browser gate — before the Xcode/simctl toolchain probe — so these land SKIPPED on EVERY host, including macOS. Mixing native app surfaces and web steps in one mobile context is likewise a deterministic SKIP (deferred to the A6 vocabulary work). The FAIL path (device-fixed browser config) is asserted in mocha (test/core-core.test.js), not here — fixtures must never FAIL.

tests.description: chrome isn't available on ios — SKIPPED with the supported browser named.
firefox isn't available on ios — SKIPPED.
webkit (the desktop engine) isn't available on ios — the device browser is `safari` — SKIPPED.
One context mixing a native app surface and a browser step — deterministic SKIP with a split-the-test pointer.
